Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.controller

Class ShipPlacementController

java.lang.Object
com.example.battleship.controller.ShipPlacementController
public class ShipPlacementController extends Object
Handles HU-1: letting the human player place their fleet manually, one ship at a time, by clicking a cell on their own board. The ship type to place always comes from the front of the fleet queue (so the order is fixed: aircraft carrier first, frigates last); the "R" key toggles the orientation the next ship will be placed with.

While the mouse hovers a cell on the player's own board, a flat, pale-cyan "ghost" copy of the ship about to be placed follows the cursor (see rebuildPreview()), showing the current orientation live -- so pressing "R" is immediately visible instead of being an invisible state change. The ghost turns red whenever the model (Board.canPlaceShip(com.example.battleship.model.ship.Ship)) reports that this exact placement would fail, so the player sees the invalid spot before clicking it.

Talks to the model (Board.placeShip(com.example.battleship.model.ship.Ship)) and mirrors every successful placement into the 3D scene as a Ship3D. Reports progress through ShipPlacementListener rather than holding a reference to any other controller -- see ShipPlacementAdapter for the easy way to listen for just one of its callbacks.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    private class 
    ShipPlacementController.RotateKeyHandler
    Toggles currentOrientation on the "R" key and refreshes the preview ghost so the rotation is immediately visible -- a private inner class (rather than a lambda) so the rotate behavior reads as a named, self-contained unit of keyboard handling.

  • Constructor Details

    • ShipPlacementController

      public ShipPlacementController(Board board, javafx.scene.Group boardGroup)
      Parameters:
      board - the human player's own (empty) board
      boardGroup - the 3D group representing that same board in the scene

  • Method Details

    • attachTo

      public void attachTo(javafx.scene.Node pickSurface)
      Wires this controller's mouse and keyboard handling onto pickSurface (normally the BattlefieldView3D itself). Mouse handlers go on pickSurface directly, but "R" is registered as an event filter on the whole Scene instead -- a SubScene (which is what pickSurface normally is) doesn't reliably keep keyboard focus even after requestFocus(), so listening at the scene level is what actually guarantees the key press is seen, regardless of which node currently has focus.
      Parameters:
      pickSurface - the node to listen on; must already be part of a Scene

    • detach

      public void detach()
      Stops listening for the "R" key -- call this once placement is done, so a later phase's own key handling isn't shadowed.

    • onCellClicked

      private void onCellClicked(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent event)
      A click on a player-board water cell attempts to place the current ship there; anything else is ignored.

    • onMouseMoved

      private void onMouseMoved(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent event)
      Moves the preview ghost to follow the mouse while it's over a player-board water cell, and clears it otherwise.

    • attemptPlacement

      private void attemptPlacement(int row, int column)
      Tries to place the current ship (front of remainingTypes) at (row, column). On success, mirrors it into the 3D scene, advances the queue, and notifies listeners -- either ShipPlacementListener.onShipPlaced(com.example.battleship.model.ship.Ship, com.example.battleship.view.ships.Ship3D) or, once the whole fleet is placed, ShipPlacementListener.onFleetPlacementComplete() as well. On failure, notifies ShipPlacementListener.onPlacementRejected(java.lang.String) instead, and nothing about the board or queue changes.

    • rebuildPreview

      private void rebuildPreview()
      Rebuilds the preview ghost at previewRow/previewColumn for the current ship type and orientation. Called on every mouse move over a new cell, every "R" press, and after every placement (since the next ship in the queue is a different type/size).

      Always asks the model (Board.canPlaceShip(com.example.battleship.model.ship.Ship)) whether this exact placement would actually succeed, and colors the ghost accordingly -- cyan if it fits, red if it would run off the board or overlap another ship.

    • clearPreview

      private void clearPreview()
      Forgets the currently hovered cell and removes the preview ghost, if there is one.

    • removePreviewNode

      private void removePreviewNode()
      Removes the current preview ghost node from the scene, if one is showing.

    • isComplete

      public boolean isComplete()
      Returns:
      true once every ship in the fleet has been placed

    • getCurrentShipType

      public ShipType getCurrentShipType()
      Returns:
      the type of the next ship to be placed, or null once the fleet is complete

    • getCurrentOrientation

      public Orientation getCurrentOrientation()
      Returns:
      the orientation the next ship will be placed with

    • getShipViews

      public Map<Ship,Ship3D> getShipViews()
      Returns:
      every ship placed so far, mapped to its 3D shape, in placement order

    • addListener

      public void addListener(ShipPlacementListener listener)
      Parameters:
      listener - a listener to notify of future placement progress

    • removeListener

      public void removeListener(ShipPlacementListener listener)
      Parameters:
      listener - a previously added listener to stop notifying
